channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Joe Marsh Extends Time as T1 CEO to 2029
item.content:encoded:
Korean reports have confirmed that the T1 board has extended Joe Marsh’s time as T1 Chief Executive Officer through to March 30 2029.

SK Telecom CS T1, the legal name of T1, held a board meeting on March 16, where the proposal to renew Marsh’s time as CEO was discussed. The motion was later passed on April 3, formally extending the American’s stay at the top of League of Legends’ most-celebrated organization.

Joe Marsh set for 10-year stay as T1 CEO

The extension means that Marsh will complete a 10-year stay at the helm of T1, after assuming the position upon the merger of SK Telecom T1 and Comcast Spectacor, where Marsh served as Chief Financial Officer (CFO) and Chief Business Officer (CBO).

The news comes shortly after the announcement that T1 had turned a profit for the first time in its organizational history, showing strong growth in Korea and foreign markets.

The organization also benefited from strong sponsorship growth, notably partnering with Saudi real-estate developer, Red Sea Global, in July of last year.

In that time, T1 has won Worlds three times, completing a historic threepeat in 2025, claiming their third Worlds title in a row with victory over KT Rolster.

Marsh’s Gumayusi intervention

Marsh’s contribution to T1’s threepeat was not merely in the background. The T1 CEO intervened to restore Lee ‘Gumayusi’ Min-hyeong to the roster following his 2025 benching in favour of Shin ‘Smash’ Geum-jae

In a statement regarding the Smash and Gumayusi situation, Marsh noted his actions, stating, “I have complete trust in Becker [General Manager], KkOma [then Head Coach], and the coaching staff, and after extensive conversations with them, I have requested that Gumayusi be part of the starting lineup and as a result, he will be starting when the regular season begins.

The T1 CEO added, “This decision was not made lightly, but as CEO, it is my responsibility to take a long-term view and ensure the best interests of T1.”

In the end, Marsh’s decision was vindicated with Gumayusi recapturing his form, ultimately earning the MVP award for the Worlds 2025 Finals.

Following Gumayusi’s departure from the org at the end of 2025, T1 turned to former Gen.G ADC, Kim ‘Peyz’ Su-hwan, who has since smashed the LCK Pentakill record in a resurgent T1.

T1 currently sits second in the LCK Regular Season standings, with the roster set to play in the Road to MSI from June 6 onwards. The roster has also qualified for the Esports World Cup 2026, which the team won in 2024.

You can discuss all things League of Legends on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see everything that was revealed on Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/joe-marsh-t1-extended-2029/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Riot Games Adds Second Stage to Berlin Studios, Preventing VCT and LEC Clashes
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has announced the addition of another stage at the Riot Games Arena in Berlin. The ‘Studio Stage’ will accommodate VCT EMEA Stage 2, allowing VCT and LEC to run concurrently in a dual-stage configuration.

The ‘Studio Stage’ will supplement the existing ‘Arena Stage’ to prevent clashes between the European Valorant and League of Legends ecosystems, allowing both leagues to operate in the same venue.

Studio Stage to adapt to VCT EMEA Stage 2 demands

The addition of the Studio Stage comes as VCT EMEA Stage 2 prepares to welcome four teams from the Tier 2 Valorant Challenger League (VCL) circuit, increasing the total teams in the split to 16.

The larger team list means an increased scheduling burden for VCT EMEA, with Riot opting to create the second stage to allow for weekend matches in the VCT.

The Studio Stage will act as the place of competition for VCT EMEA Stage 2 until it reaches the playoffs. The first week of the playoffs will then be held at the Arena Stage, before the VCT EMEA Stage 2 Finals are held in Barcelona.

In contrast to the Arena Stage, the Studio Stage cannot accommodate live fans. Riot Games acknowledged the lack of fans at the Studio Stage as a detriment, but claimed that the move will benefit later in-person events.

Riot stated, “We know live audiences are a big part of what makes the Riot Games Arena special, and that some fans will be disappointed not to attend the Studio Stage matches in person. At the same time, creating a dedicated broadcast space ensures fans won’t miss a minute online, while allowing us to increase live event investment across both products.”

Riot Games pointed to the VCT EMEA Stage 2 Finals in Barcelona as a beneficiary of the move, stating, “Most notably, this unlocks a major step forward for VCT EMEA – taking Finals on the road for the first time, with Stage 2 Finals heading to Barcelona. Alongside continued support for much-loved LEC Roadtrips, these investments will help us reach significantly more fans in person across the region.”

Could the second stage at the Riot Games Berlin Studio help the LEC?

While the second stage is due to accommodate an enlarged VCT split, even in its usual 12-team configuration, fans have been critical of the time VCT takes in reference to the League of Legends ecosystem.

As the two leagues have had to run at separate times from one another, Western League of Legends fans have been critical of the inability of the LEC to expand the number of officials European teams play each year, which severely lags behind League of Legends’ dominant leagues, the Korean LCK and the Chinese LPL.

So while the news ostensibly pertains to VCT EMEA, one wonders whether it will be the LEC that truly benefits as 2026 becomes 2027.

Would you like to see the LEC change its scheduling? Let us know on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see everything that was revealed on the Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: ARC Raiders Finally Wins a Game of the Year Award
item.content:encoded:
The Nordic Game Awards 2026 have come to a close, and the ARC Raiders have finally won a Game of the Year award.

Embark Studios’ breakout extraction shooter, ARC Raiders, has garnered several awards since its release, primarily for its multiplayer features. The game has been nominated for Game of the Year at prestigious award ceremonies, including BAFTA, DICE, and The Game Awards, but has not secured the top honor at these major events. However, the Nordic Game Awards recently changed that for them.

All Nordic Game Awards 2026 Winners

The Nordic Game Awards 2026 announced this year’s winners in a press release shared with Insider Gaming. ARC Raiders emerged as the big winner, taking home the prestigious Nordic Game of the Year award along with honors for Best Audio and Best Technology. Other notable winners included Split Fiction and LEGO Voyagers. Interested readers can find the complete list of winners for the Nordic Game Awards 2026 below:

  • Nordic Game of the Year: ARC Raiders, by Embark Studios (Sweden)
  • Best Art: The Midnight Walk, by MoonHood  (Sweden)
  • Best Game Design: Split Fiction, by Hazelight Studios (Sweden)
  • Best Technology: ARC Raiders, by Embark Studios (Sweden)
  • Best Audio: ARC Raiders, by Embark Studios (Sweden)
  • Best Fun for Everyone: LEGO Voyagers, by Light Brick Studio (Denmark)
  • Best Debut: Discounty, by Crinkle Cut Games (Denmark)

The Nordic Game Awards are hosted every spring, where not only games from Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, and Sweden are honored, but also developers, publishers, investors, students, and creatives from these regions get to network, learn, and find business opportunities. This year’s award show developers from Sweden take home the most honors.

Many fans were disappointed that ARC Raiders did not win Game of the Year (GOTY) at any major award ceremony, as the game was widely regarded as one of the best extraction shooters in recent years. However, Embark Studios has finally received a GOTY award, bringing more recognition for their efforts on the title.

In other news, the first batch of changes for Ermal has arrived, and his prices have been adjusted. Additionally, A new content roadmap for ARC Raiders is arriving “very shortly” with more details than previous versions. What are your thoughts on ARC Raiders winning Game of the Year? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Rayman Legends Retold remake price surfacing online. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/arc-raiders-finally-wins-a-game-of-the-year-award/

item.title: FIFA Reveals Long-Term Digital Football Strategy Ahead Of 2026 World Cup
item.content:encoded:
FIFA has officially unveiled its updated Digital Football Strategy, confirming ambitious plans to expand its presence across gaming, esports, and digital platforms through partnerships with multiple publishers and developers.

Rather than returning to the traditional model of one flagship football game, FIFA is now positioning itself as the centre of a much broader football gaming ecosystem. The organisation says the strategy is designed to connect partners, platforms, competitions, and fans ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 and beyond.

According to FIFA, the new strategy will see the organisation move away from a single-partner model and instead become what it describes as a “multi-partner ecosystem orchestrator.” The aim is to expand FIFA’s digital footprint across different gaming genres, platforms, and audiences while creating new ways for fans to engage with football.

At the heart of this vision lies four categories that FIFA claim each of its partnered games can fit into, including:

  • Football Action Simulation
  • Football Action Non-Simulation
  • Football Non-Action Simulation
  • Football Non-Action Non-Simulation

So far, FIFA has partnered with seven titles that form the Digital Football Ecosystem, with the upcoming Netflix game developed by Delphi Interactive at the heart of the movement and finally receiving a name: FIFA World Cup. It’s reportedly still scheduled to release in June this year, with continued expansions across consoles, PC and mobile planned.

The full line-up of FIFA partnered titles include:

  • Football Manager 26 (licensed FIFA events included)
  • eFootball (partnered for official FIFAe esports tournaments)
  • FIFA Rivals (mobile game released in 2025)
  • FIFA World Cup (upcoming Netflix Games title)
  • FIFA Heroes (upcoming mobile arcade title)
  • Rocket League (partnered for FIFAe)
  • FIFA Super Soccer (Roblox title)

What do you make of FIFA’s new strategy? Will any of these titles rival the beast that is EA Sports FC? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server. If you’re interested in regular news updates regarding the football gaming scene, check out FGZ.

For more gaming news, read about the recent trademark made by Activision for Crash Bandicoot, as well as Infinity Ward’s plans for cosmetics in Modern Warfare 4.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Will Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Have Zombies?
item.content:encoded:
Modern Warfare 4 has been officially unveiled as the next entry in the Call of Duty franchise, and questions are already beginning to circle on whether the 2026 entry features a Zombies mode.

Zombies has become a fan-favourite mode in the Call of Duty franchise, evolving from the traditional round-based mode to an open-world extraction shooter, and has become an increasingly significant part of the most recent games.

Naturally, fans are wondering whether Zombies will return in Modern Warfare 4, and Insider Gaming has all the details.

Will Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Have Zombies?

No, Modern Warfare 4 will not feature a Zombies game mode of any sort, including a round-based option or an extraction mode, like MWZ.

Zombies has become synonymous with the Call of Duty franchise, and the past three titles have all featured Zombies content in some aspect, with the past two Black Ops titles having both round-based mode and PvE antics, while Modern Warfare 3 featured the inaugural MWZ mode.

  • More Insider Gaming: Will Modern Warfare 4 be on Game Pass?

MWZ in Modern Warfare 3 took place on a huge map, splitting zones into three areas of difficulty, and while it wasn’t for many players, it established a popular fanbase and offered a different experience after years of round-based Zombies action.

In Black Ops 6, round-based Zombies was the focus, while Black Ops 6 had the same round-based approach, alongside the open-world ‘Endgame’ mode. However, that trend comes to an end with Modern Warfare 4.

What Game Modes Will Modern Warfare 4 Have?

Modern Warfare 4 will feature the game modes everyone has come to love, with the main story, multiplayer content, Warzone, and the return of DMZ.

DMZ acts as a replacement for Zombies in Modern Warfare 4, returning to the Call of Duty franchise for the first time since Modern Warfare 2. While MWZ was somewhat of a successor to DMZ, Modern Warfare 4 returns to a more grounded reality.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Modern Warfare 4 Pre-Order Prices, Editions & Rewards

Rather than facing off against Zombies, players in DMZ will experience a PvPvE environment, with the choice of taking down AI-controlled enemies and objectives, going head-to-head with enemy players, or a mix of both.

Activision says DMZ is back and ‘bigger than ever’ in Modern Warfare 4, and more details on the mode will be revealed as the launch window edges closer.

Are you excited for Modern Warfare 4? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Modern Warfare 4, check out the details of the Prestige system, and take a look at the return of DMZ.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/modern-warfare-4-zombies/

item.title: Rayman Legends Retold Price Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has provided the price for Rayman Legends Retold, along with some other details such as online multiplayer.

For those unaware, just a few days ago, a new logo appeared for Rayman Legends Remake, which has been titled ‘Rayman Legends Retold’. Insider Gaming’s report also confirmed that the game was scheduled for release on October 1, 2026. Now details such as price, physical edition, and more have been revealed.

Rayman Legends Retold Will Cost $40 and Will Have a Physical Edition

The latest details about Rayman Legends Retold’s price and more come from a report by billbil-kun on Dealabs. According to their information, they can also confirm the October 1, 2026, release date for all platforms and have added that the game will receive a physical edition that is exclusive to PS5 and Switch 2. Rayman Legends Retold’s price has been set as $39.99 in the United States.

The other information shared indicates that the Rayman Legends Remake is being developed by Ubisoft Montpellier, not in Milan. The game will feature an online multiplayer mode that allows 4 players to play simultaneously. The game will also come with a launch edition that includes additional bonuses.

Announcement Expected on June 2

The official announcement for the game is seemingly planned for June 2, 2026, which is also the day PlayStation will host its State of Play event. However, billbil-kun isn’t absolutely confident about this information, so players should treat it with caution. According to their information, the announcement is expected to arrive a few hours before State of Play, and they will open pre-orders at the same time.

In other news, Ubisoft has confirmed it reduced its employee count by over 1,000 over the last year. What are your thoughts on Rayman Legends Retold’s price, multiplayer, and other details? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Activision filing a trademark for Crash Bandicoot motion pitcures. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/rayman-legends-retold-price-revealed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Will Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Be On XBOX Game Pass?
item.content:encoded:
Like death and taxes, a new Call of Duty title is inevitable every year, and the 2026 edition of the franchise, Modern Warfare 4, has now been revealed, and many players are questioning the Game Pass status.

XBOX Game Pass has proven to be a solid value for money for gamers, offering day-one access to a host of titles when they launch, including many Microsoft first-party games, but some changes have been made.

If you’re wondering whether Modern Warfare 4 will be on Game Pass, or if you’ll have to shell out separately, Insider Gaming has all the important details.

Will Modern Warfare 4 be on XBOX Game Pass?

No, Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 will not be available on the XBOX Game Pass catalog at launch, though it will be added at a later date.

A host of details for Modern Warfare 4 have been released by Activision, who have made it explicitly clear that the title will not be available on Game Pass at launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: Modern Warfare 4 Cosmetics & Collabs Will Be ‘Grounded’

This is a major shift for the franchise after back-to-back Call of Duty games launching day one on the Game Pass service, following Microsoft’s takeover of Activision Blizzard, and may come as a shock for those out of the loop.

The good news, however, is that the door isn’t shut on Modern Warfare 4 being on Game Pass permanently, and the title will be added within a year of its launch.

Why Won’t Modern Warfare 4 be on XBOX Game Pass?

Microsoft changed a significant amount of details in April’s update to the XBOX Game Pass service, where it was announced that Call of Duty titles will not be available day one on the service.

Instead, games from the franchise will be added in the following year’s holiday season, around 12 months after launch. While this is still good news, Call of Duty fans will know that the franchise has a yearly release cycle, so playing solely through Game Pass will mean you will always be one entry behind.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Modern Warfare 4 Pre-Order Prices, Editions & Rewards

While XBOX did not outline the specifics behind the reasons for the change to Call of Duty titles, including the game in the service will have cost the company a significant amount of money compared to what they could have earned by selling the game to all players.

It does leave the Call of Duty franchise in a strange situation, however, as it currently stands as the only first-party Microsoft franchise not to be set for day one additions to Game Pass.

Are you excited for Modern Warfare 4? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Modern Warfare 4, check out the details of the Prestige system, and take a look at the return of DMZ.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/modern-warfare-4-game-pass/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Pre-Order Prices, Editions & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
The next era of the Call of Duty franchise has been revealed with the unveiling of Modern Warfare 4, and pre-orders are available now, offering a host of additional rewards.

After back-to-back Black Ops titles, Call of Duty is returning to the Modern Warfare saga for the first time since 2023, marking the fourth entry in Activision‘s revamped Modern Warfare saga.

If you’re keen on pre-ordering the game and want all of the available details, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All Modern Warfare 4 Pre-Order Prices

Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 is available for pre-order now, with a minimum price of $69.99. However, there are ways to earn discounts.

Xbox is offering loyalty bonuses for Modern Warfare 4 on the Vault Edition, saving up to $10, but that still requires forking out a hefty asking price.

  • More Insider Gaming: Modern Warfare 4 Cosmetics & Collabs Will Be ‘Grounded’

However, with that bonus, the Vault Edition is only $20 more expensive than the Standard Edition, so passionate fans of the franchise may want to secure everything available. An upgrade to the Vault Edition is available for $30.

The table below shows all of the editions available for Modern Warfare 4, and the current prices for pre-order.

All Modern Warfare 4 Editions & Bonuses

Any player who pre-orders Modern Warfare 4 earns early access to the Open Beta, along with the Hunter Killer Operator skin.

Details of the duration and launch of the open beta are yet to be revealed, but by pre-ordering either version of Modern Warfare 4, players will secure their spot at the front of the queue.

Meanwhile, the Hunter Killer Operator skin is a pre-order reward that is available immediately, which can be used in Warzone and Black Ops 6, along with Modern Warfare 4 when it launches.

Check out the table below for all the pre-order bonuses and the editions they come with.

Are you excited for Modern Warfare 4? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Modern Warfare 4, check out the details of the Prestige system, and take a look at the return of DMZ.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
